title: Listen Up
tagline: Big books, distilled. The key ideas and the stories that make them stick — in about 15 minutes.

books.title: Atomic Habits
books.author: James Clear
books.blurb: Tiny 1% changes compound into remarkable results. The Four Laws for building good habits and breaking bad ones — with the British-cycling and two-minute-rule examples.

books.title: The Psychology of Money
books.author: Morgan Housel
books.blurb: Doing well with money is about behaviour, not brains. Luck vs risk, knowing what's 'enough', and why compounding rewards the patient — through Gates, Buffett and the man-in-the-car paradox.
chapters: Wealth is what you don't see

books.title: Deep Work
books.author: Cal Newport
books.blurb: Focused, distraction-free work is a superpower that's getting rarer. How to train deep concentration and get more done in less time.

books.title: The 7 Habits of Highly Effective People
books.author: Stephen Covey
books.blurb: The classic principle-centred framework: be proactive, begin with the end in mind, put first things first, and sharpen the saw.

books.title: The Almanack of Naval Ravikant
books.author: Eric Jorgenson
books.blurb: Naval's playbook on wealth and happiness: build specific knowledge, earn with your mind not your time, and play long-term games with long-term people.

books.title: Rich Dad Poor Dad
books.author: Robert Kiyosaki
books.blurb: Assets vs liabilities, making money work for you, and why financial education matters more than a high salary.

books.title: Why We Sleep
books.author: Matthew Walker
books.blurb: Sleep is the single most effective thing you can do to reset brain and body. What it does, and what happens when you skimp on it.

books.title: Outlive
books.author: Peter Attia
books.blurb: The science and art of longevity: how to add healthy years, not just years, by training for the marginal decade.

books.title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
books.author: Daniel Kahneman
books.blurb: Two systems drive the way we think — fast and intuitive, slow and deliberate — and the biases that quietly steer our decisions.

books.title: Sapiens
books.author: Yuval Noah Harari
books.blurb: How an unremarkable ape came to rule the planet — through shared fictions, agriculture, money, empires and science.
